tools.description: Add language targeting to a campaign.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to add language targeting to.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

language_constant_ids.description: List of language constant IDs (e.g., ["1005"] for Japanese).

tools.name: add_campaign_language

tools.description: Add location (geo) targeting to a campaign.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to add location targeting to.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

geo_target_constant_ids.description: List of geo target constant IDs (e.g., ["2392"] for Japan).

tools.name: add_campaign_location

tools.description: Add keywords to an ad group.

ad_group_id.description: The ad group ID to add keywords to.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

keywords.description: List of keyword objects, each with "text" (str) and "match_type" ("EXACT", "PHRASE", or "BROAD").

items.description: A keyword with text and match type.

tools.name: add_keywords

tools.description: Add negative keywords to a campaign.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to add negative keywords to.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

keywords.description: List of keyword objects, each with "text" (str) and "match_type" ("EXACT", "PHRASE", or "BROAD").

items.description: A keyword with text and match type.

tools.name: add_negative_keywords

tools.description:
Add negative keywords to a shared negative keyword list.

Shared sets are account-level negative keyword lists that can be linked
to multiple campaigns. Use the ``search`` tool with resource
``shared_set`` (conditions: ``shared_set.type = 'NEGATIVE_KEYWORDS'``)
to find shared set IDs and names.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

keywords.description: List of keyword objects, each with "text" (str) and "match_type" ("EXACT", "PHRASE", or "BROAD").

items.description: A keyword with text and match type.

shared_set_id.description: The shared set ID to add negative keywords to.

tools.name: add_shared_set_negative_keywords

tools.description: Create a new ad group in PAUSED state.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to create the ad group in.

cpc_bid_micros.description: CPC bid in micros (e.g., 1000000 = $1.00 CPC). Must be positive.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: create_ad_group

tools.description:
Create a new Performance Max asset group in PAUSED state.

The asset group is the container that holds all creative assets (images,
text, logos) for a P-MAX campaign.

Google Ads requires the asset group and its required assets to be created
in a single atomic ``mutate`` call. Pass the full set of pre-created assets
(created via ``upload_image_asset`` and ``create_text_asset``) via the
``assets`` parameter so the asset group is created with all required slots
filled. Additional assets can be attached later via
``link_assets_to_asset_group``.

items.description: An asset to link to an asset group with a specific field type.

assets.description:
List of {"asset_id": "...", "field_type": "..."} entries to
link to the asset group at creation time. Optional; if omitted,
the asset group will be created without any linked assets and
the API will reject it unless the minimum requirements are met.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: create_asset_group

tools.description:
Create a new campaign in PAUSED state with a new daily budget.

The campaign is created with MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS bidding (uncapped)
and standard network settings (Google Search + Search Partners).

budget_amount_micros.description: Daily budget in micros (e.g., 50000000 = $50/day). Must be positive.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: create_campaign

tools.description:
Create a new Performance Max campaign in PAUSED state with a new daily budget.

Performance Max campaigns auto-target Search, Display, YouTube, Discover,
Gmail, and Maps using the supplied asset group(s). The campaign is created
with MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS bidding (uncapped). Asset groups must be created
separately via ``create_asset_group`` and populated via ``upload_image_asset``,
``create_text_asset``, and ``link_assets_to_asset_group``.

brand_guidelines_enabled.description: Whether to enable Brand Guidelines on the campaign. Defaults to False.

budget_amount_micros.description: Daily budget in micros (e.g., 3000000000 = ¥3,000/day). Must be positive.

items.description: An asset to link to an asset group with a specific field type.

campaign_assets.description:
List of {"asset_id": "...", "field_type": "..."} entries
to attach as CampaignAsset entries atomically with the campaign.
Required when ``brand_guidelines_enabled`` is True (at least one
BUSINESS_NAME and one LOGO).

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: create_pmax_campaign

tools.description: Create a new responsive search ad (RSA) in PAUSED state.

ad_group_id.description: The ad group ID to create the ad in.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

descriptions.description: List of description texts (min 2, max 4). Each description max 90 characters.

headlines.description: List of headline texts (min 3, max 15). Each headline max 30 characters.

path1.description: Optional display URL path 1 (max 15 characters, e.g., "products").

path2.description: Optional display URL path 2 (max 15 characters, e.g., "shoes"). Only used if path1 is set.

tools.name: create_responsive_search_ad

tools.description:
Create a reusable text asset (used for HEADLINE / LONG_HEADLINE / DESCRIPTION / BUSINESS_NAME / CALLOUT).

The returned asset ID can be linked to a P-MAX asset group via
``link_assets_to_asset_group`` with the appropriate text field type.
Length limits depend on field type at link time:
  - HEADLINE: max 30 chars (display width)
  - LONG_HEADLINE: max 90 chars
  - DESCRIPTION: max 90 chars (or 60 for the short slot)
  - BUSINESS_NAME: max 25 chars
  - CALLOUT: max 25 chars

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: create_text_asset

tools.description:
Retrieves the selectable, filterable, and sortable fields for a specific Google Ads resource,
including compatible metrics and segments.

Use this tool to find out which fields you can select, filter by, or sort by
when querying a specific resource (e.g., 'campaign', 'ad_group').
This tool also returns metrics and segments that can be selected with the resource.
Their names start with 'metrics.' and 'segments.' respectively.

Do not guess fields, you MUST use this tool to discover them before constructing a query for the
`search` tool.

The responses of this tool should be cached, as they don't change frequently.

resource_name.description: The name of the Google Ads resource (e.g., 'campaign', 'ad_group').

tools.name: get_resource_metadata

tools.description:
Link existing assets to a Performance Max asset group with a field type.

Each entry attaches a single asset (created by ``upload_image_asset`` or
``create_text_asset``) to the asset group as the given creative slot.

asset_group_id.description: The asset group ID to link assets to.

items.description: An asset to link to an asset group with a specific field type.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: link_assets_to_asset_group

tools.description:
Link existing assets to a campaign with a field type (CampaignAsset).

Required for Performance Max campaigns with Brand Guidelines enabled,
which mandate at least one BUSINESS_NAME and one LOGO linked at the
campaign level (CampaignAsset, not AssetGroupAsset).

items.description: An asset to link to an asset group with a specific field type.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to link assets to.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: link_assets_to_campaign

tools.description:
Link a shared set (e.g., negative keyword list) to a campaign.

Use the ``search`` tool with resource ``shared_set`` to find
shared set IDs and names.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to link the shared set to.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

shared_set_id.description: The shared set ID to link.

tools.name: link_shared_set_to_campaign

tools.description:
Returns ids of customers directly accessible by the user authenticating the call.

Use this tool first to discover available customer IDs if the user hasn't
provided one. Most other tools require a valid customer ID as input.

Returns:
    List[str]: A list of customer IDs.
tools.name: list_accessible_customers

tools.description:
Fetches data from the Google Ads API using the search method

    Args:
        customer_id: The id of the customer
        fields: The fields to fetch
        resource: The resource to return fields from
        conditions: List of conditions to filter the data, combined using AND clauses
        orderings: How the data is ordered
        limit: The maximum number of rows to return

    

### Hints
    Language Grammar can be found at https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/query/grammar
    All resources and descriptions are found at https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/fields/latest/overview
    If the query fails, a ToolError will be raised with the error details.

    For Conversion issues try looking in offline_conversion_upload_conversion_action_summary

### Hint for customer_id
    should be a string of numbers without punctuation
    if presented in the form 123-456-7890 remove the hyphens and use 1234567890

### Hints for Dates
    All dates should be in the form YYYY-MM-DD and must include the dashes (-)
    Date ranges must be finite and must include a start and end date

### Hints for limits
    Requests to resource change_event must specify a LIMIT of less than or equal to 10000

### Hints for conversions questions
    https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/upload-summaries 


### Hints for all resources
    To find out which specific fields (including compatible metrics and segments) you can select, filter by, or sort by for a given resource, you MUST use the `get_resource_metadata` tool.
    Do not guess the fields. Use the tool to look them up.
    Once you have the fields, ensure the whole field name is used (e.g., 'campaign.id', not just 'id'). Wildcards and partial fields are not allowed.

### Valid resources
    What follows is a list of valid resources that can be queried.
    accessible_bidding_strategy
account_budget
account_budget_proposal
account_link
ad
ad_group
ad_group_ad

tools.name: search

tools.description:
Set ad schedules for a campaign. This replaces all existing ad schedules.

Each schedule defines a day and time window when ads should run.
To run ads Monday-Friday all day, pass 5 schedule entries (one per day)
with start_hour=0, end_hour=24.

campaign_id.description: The campaign ID to set schedules for.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

schedules.description:
List of schedule objects, each with:
- "day_of_week": "MONDAY"|"TUESDAY"|"WEDNESDAY"|"THURSDAY"|"FRIDAY"|"SATURDAY"|"SUNDAY"
- "start_hour": 0-23
- "start_minute": "ZERO"|"FIFTEEN"|"THIRTY"|"FORTY_FIVE" (default: "ZERO")
- "end_hour": 0-24 (24 means end of day)
- "end_minute": "ZERO"|"FIFTEEN"|"THIRTY"|"FORTY_FIVE" (default: "ZERO")

items.description: An ad schedule entry defining a day and time window.

tools.name: set_ad_schedule

tools.description: Update an ad's final URL.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_ad_final_url

ad_group_id.description: The ad group ID to update.

cpc_bid_micros.description: New CPC bid in micros (e.g., 1500000 = $1.50 CPC). Must be positive.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_ad_group_bid

tools.description: Update an ad group's status (ENABLED or PAUSED).

ad_group_id.description: The ad group ID to update.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_ad_group_status

tools.description: Update an ad's status (ENABLED or PAUSED).

ad_group_id.description: The ad group ID containing the ad.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_ad_status

tools.description: Update a Performance Max asset group's final URL.

asset_group_id.description: The asset group ID to update.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_asset_group_final_url

tools.description: Update a Performance Max asset group's status (ENABLED or PAUSED).

asset_group_id.description: The asset group ID to update.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_asset_group_status

tools.description: Update a campaign's bidding strategy.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

target_roas.description: Target ROAS as a float (required for TARGET_ROAS, e.g., 4.0 = 400% ROAS). Optional for MAXIMIZE_CONVERSION_VALUE (uncapped if omitted).

target_value_micros.description: Target CPA in micros (required for TARGET_CPA, e.g., 5000000 = $5). Optional for MAXIMIZE_CONVERSIONS (uncapped if omitted).

tools.name: update_bidding_strategy

tools.description:
Toggle Brand Guidelines on a Performance Max campaign.

When ``enabled`` is True, the campaign will require at least one
BUSINESS_NAME and one LOGO linked at the campaign level
(CampaignAsset). Use ``link_assets_to_campaign`` BEFORE enabling if
those assets are not already attached as CampaignAsset entries.

When ``enabled`` is False, BUSINESS_NAME and LOGO must instead be
attached as AssetGroupAsset entries on every asset group.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

enabled.description: True to enable Brand Guidelines, False to disable.

tools.name: update_campaign_brand_guidelines

tools.description:
Update a campaign budget amount. Amount is in micros (1 currency unit = 1,000,000 micros).

For example, to set a daily budget of $50, pass amount_micros=50000000.

amount_micros.description: New budget amount in micros (e.g., 50000000 = $50). Must be positive.

budget_id.description: The campaign budget ID to update.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_campaign_budget

tools.description:
Update a campaign conversion goal's biddable setting.

Controls whether the campaign's automated bidding strategy optimizes
for this conversion goal.

biddable.description: Whether the bidding strategy should optimize for this goal.

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_campaign_conversion_goal

tools.description: Update a campaign's status (ENABLED or PAUSED).

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

tools.name: update_campaign_status

tools.description:
Upload a local image file as a Google Ads image asset.

The returned asset ID can be linked to a P-MAX asset group via
``link_assets_to_asset_group`` with an image field type
(MARKETING_IMAGE / SQUARE_MARKETING_IMAGE / PORTRAIT_MARKETING_IMAGE /
LOGO / LANDSCAPE_LOGO).

customer_id.description: The Google Ads customer ID (digits only, no dashes).

name.description: Asset display name (used to identify the asset in the UI).

tools.name: upload_image_asset
